quote: the rest having gone with her brother Moredo to the Vale
Lady Clarice Osgrey, widowed aunt of Lord Unwin Peake
she had often shared her bed with Ser Mervyn Flowers
at Ser Mervyn's behest with Tessario the Tiger
The fool had grown fond of the big northman

text: When Rowan's tortured confession was heard he was left bereft of speech
It fell to Prince Viserys to answer the confession, and by mock-questions he exposed it as false, drawing Rowan into confessing that Gaemon and Viserys himself were among the guilty
She wept when Lord Torrhen handed down his verdict on her brothers, and later bore Viserys a second son and disciplined her elder son
The fool claims the queen's ladies donned mail to feign more defenders
In his *Testimony* he names Unwin Peake as the hidden "player in the shadows"
Under torture he named Marston Waters, then Graceford, then Flowers, dying before a fourth name
Ser Lucas Leygood commanded the gold cloaks, more than two thousand strong, and was named among the plotters
He judged the treason trials, reformed the taxes with Isembard Arryn's help, filled the Kingsguard, and planned a royal progress
He left for White Harbor an enemy of the king
Lady Clarice, widowed aunt of Unwin Peake, supervised Queen Daenaera's attendants as she had Queen Jaehaera's before
Ser Raynard was the one Kingsguard within the holdfast, held prisoner after being wounded by the Lyseni
